texto: The thought crossed my mind that this could be dangerous. Not the ill-advised sex with the just-out-of-his-teens pop star, but the cuddling. The lying there, drinking in his scent, watching his chest rise and fall, allowing myself to bask in my own happiness. I could fall in love this way.
explicacao: Citação de Robinne Lee sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “The thought crossed my mind that this could be dangerous. Not the ill-advised sex with the just-o…” articula reflexao, falling-in-love, could em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: The thought crossed my mind that this could be dangerous. Not the ill-advised sex with the just-out-of-his-teens pop star, but the cuddling. The lying there, drinking in his scent, watching his chest rise and fall, allowing myself to bask in my own happiness. I could fall in love this way.

texto: I’m really happy when I’m with you. I get the feeling you feel the same way. And if that’s true, I don’t think you should give a fuck about what people may or may not think of our age difference. Furthermore, if our ages were reversed, no one would bat an eyelash. Am I right? So now it’s just some sexist, patriarchal crap, and you don’t strike me as the kind of woman who’s going to let that dictate her happiness. All right? Next issue…
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Robinne Lee que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “I’m really happy when I’m with you. I get the feeling you feel the same way. And if that’s true, …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, ageism, lovers, com foco em lovers e women-s-strength.

So now it’s just some sexist, patriarchal crap, and you don’t strike me as the kind of woman who’s going to let that dictate her happiness. All right? Next issue…

texto: This is insane. You realize that, right?” “Only if someone gets hurt.” “Someone always gets hurt, Hayes.” He said nothing as he slid his fingers in between mine, squeezing my hand. The intimacy of the gesture threw me. I had not held a man’s hand since Daniel’s, and Hayes’s felt foreign. Large, smooth, capable; the coolness of an unexpected ring.I shifted in my skirt, legs sticking to the leather cushion. I needed to get out of there, and yet I did not want it to end.
